hawkish_phrases: underlying price pressures continued to lack a convincing upward trend and remained an ongoing source of concern
no clear upward trend in inflation was as yet visible
it was premature to discuss any possible monetary policy reaction at this stage
market-based measures of long-term inflation expectations...had been steadily declining for some time and remained close to record lows
inflation continued to be significantly below the Governing Council's policy aim
dovish_phrases: downside risks to the economic outlook for the euro area had increased, mainly owing to the outcome of the UK referendum
the Governing Council needed to reiterate its capacity and readiness to act, if warranted, to achieve its objective, using all the instruments available within its mandate
continue to monitor economic and financial market developments very closely
if warranted to achieve its objective of price stability, it would act by using all the instruments available within its mandate
more time was needed to assess the incoming information over the coming months, although downside risks had clearly increased
summary: The Governing Council held rates unchanged at 0.00% (main refinancing), 0.25% (marginal lending), and -0.40% (deposit facility) with unanimous support, while acknowledging increased downside risks from the UK referendum and reiterating readiness to act if warranted, though deeming it premature to discuss policy changes.
